name: Northern Homes Community Development Corporation
description_short: Non-profit Community Development Corporation offering free homeownership counseling, affordable housing assistance, and financial guidance to Northern Michigan residents since 1998.
description_long:
Northern Homes Community Development Corporation is a non-profit organization established in 1998 dedicated to empowering individuals and families across Northern Michigan to achieve homeownership. Operating in Emmet, Cheboygan, Charlevoix, Antrim, Otsego, Crawford, and surrounding areas, the organization has built a 25-year track record of community engagement and financial education.

The organization provides a comprehensive suite of free services including homeownership counseling, financial fitness guidance, affordable housing placement, and specialized foreclosure assistance. They offer free educational workshops covering credit scores, mortgage navigation, budgeting, and renting versus buying decisions. Additionally, they provide information on loan products like USDA 502 loans and Community Land Trusts, with personalized individual counseling sessions available to prospective homebuyers.

Northern Homes distinguishes itself through direct affordable housing placement connecting clients with single-family homes, multi-family properties, and apartments tailored to individual budgets. Their foreclosure assistance program specifically targets homeowners in distress, offering resources and negotiation support. The organization also conducts community outreach, delivering financial literacy education on topics like predatory lending and fixed-income budgeting to partner organizations and educational institutions.

As a free non-profit service, Northern Homes carries no conflicts of interest common to profit-driven lenders. However, their geographic service area is limited to Northern Michigan, and the website does not specify counselor certifications, wait times for counseling appointments, or details about loan product eligibility requirements. Consumers outside their service region or requiring immediate crisis intervention may need to seek alternative resources.
pros: Completely free services with no fees for counseling, workshops, or housing guidance
25-year operational history (since 1998) providing stability and community trust
Direct affordable housing placement with access to single-family, multi-family, and apartment options
Specialized foreclosure assistance helping homeowners facing potential loss
Personalized one-on-one counseling in addition to group workshops
Community education programs on credit, predatory lending, and budgeting delivered to schools and senior organizations
Information on loan products including USDA 502 loans and Community Land Trusts
cons: Geographic service limited to seven Northern Michigan counties (Emmet, Cheboygan, Charlevoix, Antrim, Otsego, Crawford, and surrounding areas only)
Website does not specify HUD counselor certifications, NFCC affiliations, or counselor credentials
No information on counseling appointment availability, wait times, or response timeline
Limited details on specific loan product eligibility requirements or down payment assistance programs
No indication of language accessibility or services for non-English speakers
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Northern Michigan seeking free guidance through the entire purchase process
Homeowners facing foreclosure in the service area needing negotiation support and alternatives
Low-to-moderate income families and seniors seeking affordable housing options aligned with their budget
Individuals with limited credit knowledge needing foundational education on credit scores and mortgage readiness
diagnosis: Northern Homes is best for Northern Michigan residents seeking free, non-predatory guidance on homeownership, particularly first-time buyers and those facing foreclosure. The primary limitation is geographic service area restriction to seven Northern Michigan counties.
services: Free homeownership counseling and personalized guidance
Free educational workshops on credit scores and credit building
Mortgage navigation and home-buying process education
Affordable housing placement and search assistance
Community Land Trust education and access
Community financial literacy presentations on predatory lending
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1048 E Main St, Boyne City, MI 49712
